description: Workspace, section and domain model for supporting multiple work scopes without forking the intelligence pipeline.
core_rule: Every workspace keeps the core intelligence modules: source management, candidate pool, daily reports, weekly reports, historical archive, entity timeline archive, quality archive, SQL exports, workspace settings center, users and audit. Optional modules are database-registered and disabled by default.

workspace.purpose: A user-facing work scope that still uses the same intelligence pipeline.
owns: isolated unified label policy for model tagging
feedback policy for viewer collaboration permissions

section.purpose: A database-registered core page or optional plugin page inside a workspace.

domain.purpose: The subject matter of intelligence content, such as AI, hardware, semiconductor, robotics or policy.

fields: code (^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$, unique across enabled and disabled workspaces)
provisioning: All core_sections are registered and enabled for the new workspace.
config_json.label_policy starts from the default ai_sql_categories policy and is then edited per workspace via PATCH /api/workspaces/{code}/label-policy.
config_json.feedback_policy starts from the default viewer feedback policy and is then edited per workspace via PATCH /api/workspaces/{code}/feedback-policy.
Existing super admins receive owner memberships.
The creator receives an owner membership even when the creator is editor_admin rather than super_admin.
sort_order is appended after existing workspaces.
rules: Custom workspaces are database records; the startup seed only maintains the built-in planning_intel and ai_tools definitions and must never disable or overwrite custom workspaces.
New workspaces integrate sources through the shared pool: enable existing sources via workspace_source_links or create new ones via POST /api/sources.
Creating a workspace must not fork the pipeline, the SQL contract or the taxonomy files.
Disabled workspaces are hidden from GET /api/workspaces but data is retained.
planning_intel cannot be disabled.
Startup seed maintains only built-in planning_intel and ai_tools and must not overwrite custom workspaces.

workspaces.visibility: private workspaces are member-only; internal_public workspaces are listed in GET /api/workspaces/discover for logged-in users and readable by guest sessions. The startup seed only sets the initial value on creation (planning_intel=internal_public, everything else private) and never overwrites the visibility API decision.

membership_management.min_role: workspace admin or owner; super_admin bypasses membership
rules: GET /api/workspaces filters to enabled memberships for non-super_admin users.
Member removal disables the membership row instead of deleting history.
The last enabled owner in a workspace cannot be removed.

workspace.member.upsert and workspace.member.remove audit logs must include before/after membership snapshots so /api/identity/permission-changes can explain the diff and /api/identity/permission-rollbacks can restore the previous state.
The frontend member-management view must explain role impact and disable the remove action for the last owner.

POST /api/workspaces/{workspace_code}/members/bulk expands a user_groups group_code into per-user memberships idempotently: existing enabled memberships keep their role (never downgraded), disabled memberships are re-enabled with the requested role, inactive accounts are skipped, and the requested workspace_role must be viewer/member/admin (owner requires the single-member flow with dangerous-change confirmation). The action is audited as workspace.member.bulk_upsert with added/reactivated/skipped user id lists.

GET /api/workspaces/discover lists enabled internal_public workspaces for any logged-in user with name/description/member_count/joined/workspace_role; private workspaces never appear (no existence leak).

GET /api/workspaces/discover accepts an optional q parameter (implemented 2026-07-08; backend/app/api/routes/workspaces.py, covered by backend/tests/test_workspace_join_codes.py): case-insensitive contains filter on name and description; the filter scope stays strictly enabled internal_public workspaces, so no keyword can surface a private workspace.

POST /api/workspaces/{code}/subscribe lets a logged-in user self-join an internal_public workspace as a viewer member, idempotently: an existing enabled membership keeps its role, a disabled membership is re-enabled as viewer. Private or unknown workspaces answer 404 with the same body. Audited as workspace.member.subscribe with before/after snapshots.

DELETE /api/workspaces/{code}/subscribe disables the caller own viewer membership idempotently (204 even when not subscribed); roles above viewer answer 400 and stay managed through the member-management endpoints (protects the last-owner rule). Audited as workspace.member.unsubscribe.

Guest sessions (AUTH_GUEST_ENABLED, see auth_modes.json guest_access) never hold memberships: they browse enabled internal_public workspaces through an implicit viewer perspective and every write, including subscribe, is rejected 403 by the central guest gate with a register-first hint.
The frontend discovery entry is a centered modal (frontend-product-design sections 10 and 12): search box on top (discover?q=), join-by-code block at the bottom.

visibility_matrix.description: Who sees what, per workspace visibility and deployment overlay. Answers the platform-publicity question: public platform = internal_public plus AUTH_GUEST_ENABLED (guest read-only is the publicity ceiling); team-only = private, entered via join code, invite or member management.

private.logged_in_non_member: not discoverable, direct access 404; may enter via workspace join code

internal_public.anonymous: invisible until login or guest entry
internal_public.guest_session: read-only published content through implicit viewer; every write 403 with register-first hint
internal_public.logged_in_non_member: discoverable and self-subscribable as viewer; a join code may grant the code default_role instead

join_code.status: implemented (2026-07-08): backend/app/api/routes/workspace_access.py + workspace_join_codes table/migration; covered by backend/tests/test_workspace_join_codes.py (incl. anti-enumeration and rate-limit cases). Fact source docs/backend/workspace-configuration-design.md section 14
join_code.purpose: Lightweight self-service team entry for already-registered users; complements user_invites (which target unregistered individuals, create accounts and bind global roles). Join codes never create accounts and never change global roles.

workspace_join_codes: code (str16 unique, 8 chars uppercase letters+digits excluding 0/O/1/I)

endpoints: GET /api/workspaces/{workspace_code}/join-code (workspace admin/owner; super_admin bypasses membership) -> current active code or null

POST /api/workspaces/{workspace_code}/join-code (workspace admin/owner) body {default_role?, expires_in_days?, max_uses?} -> new code; an existing active code is rotated (disabled in the same transaction)
POST /api/workspaces/join-by-code (any logged-in user; guest gate 403) body {code} -> {workspace_code, workspace_name, workspace_role, joined}
rules: At most one active join code per workspace; rotation disables the old code and creates the new one in a single transaction; historical codes are kept for audit.
default_role only accepts viewer or member; admin/owner memberships stay on the single-member management flow with dangerous-change confirmation.
join-by-code answers one uniform 400 body for unknown/disabled/expired/exhausted codes so codes cannot be enumerated and workspace existence never leaks.

Joining is idempotent and never downgrades: an enabled membership keeps its role (joined=false), a disabled membership is re-enabled with the code default_role, a new membership gets default_role; use_count increments only on a real add or re-enable.
Private and internal_public workspaces can both be joined by code (this is the team-only self-service entry); disabled workspaces behave as invalid codes.
Failed join attempts are rate limited per user+IP (10 failures / 15 minutes -> 429), mirroring the login rate-limit mechanics.

Audit actions: workspace.join_code.create (rotation logs rotated_from in detail), workspace.join_code.disable, workspace.member.join_by_code with before/after membership snapshots (same convention as workspace.member.subscribe; not part of the permission-rollback supported list).
Join codes are workspace operations config, independent from deploy-mode capability gates; they change no global role and no RBAC semantics.
UI entries: /workspace-settings visibility-and-join-code card (admin/owner) and the join-by-code block in the workspace discovery modal.

section_groups.notes: Navigation is grouped vertically by editorial job. The group lives in workspace_sections.config_json.group and is returned by the sections API; the frontend renders groups dynamically.

section_min_role.storage: workspace_sections.config_json.min_role, seeded by SECTION_MIN_ROLE_SEED and resolvable per section
section_min_role.defaults: Reading sections (daily_reports/weekly_reports/historical_reports/entity_milestones) are viewer; other sections default to member; workspace_settings is admin.
section_min_role.frontend_rule: AppShell filters navigation by the effective workspace role (global super_admin/editor_admin count as owner); the router guard redirects viewers from management routes back to /daily-reports.

rules: Optional sections can be enabled/disabled per workspace from the workspace settings center (/workspace-settings); disabling any core section answers 400.
The user decision persists in workspace_sections.config_json.user_enabled so the startup seed cannot roll it back; the change is audited as workspace.section.update with before/after enabled.
workspace_settings is a system-group core section with config_json.min_role=admin: only workspace admin/owner (or global super_admin/editor_admin) see the settings center.

workspace_sections: config_json.min_role (per-section minimum workspace role; see section_min_role)
config_json.user_enabled (admin enable/disable decision that survives startup re-seeding)

scope_fields.workspace_code: Which workspace a processed object belongs to. Data source definitions are reusable and are enabled through workspace_source_links.
scope_fields.domain_code: Which intelligence domain the content belongs to.
scope_fields.visibility_scope: Who can see or sync the object.
scope_fields.sync_policy: How the object moves between public, intranet and local deployments.

rules: Do not use domain_code as a UI workspace boundary.
Do not create a separate frontend/backend for any workspace.
Do not remove source management, candidate pool, daily reports or weekly reports from any workspace.
Do not duplicate data source definitions per workspace; reuse data_sources through workspace_source_links.
New work scopes are workspaces; optional pages/tools are workspace_sections with enabled=false by default.
The frontend must load workspace list and enabled sections from backend APIs; hard-coded navigation is only a temporary scaffold.
Do not show tool_catalog, tool_runs or standalone_topics unless workspace_sections explicitly enables them.
First-level and second-level news labels are configured once per workspace in workspaces.config_json.label_policy and constrain model-generated news categories.

planning_intel defaults to ai_sql_categories from config/taxonomy/news_categories.json. ai_tools defaults to ai_tools_categories with 工具新功能/工具新案例/工具新技术 and cursor/claude code/opencode/codex as second-level labels under each primary label.
Source-side tags use planning_source_tags from config/taxonomy/source_tags.json and are only data-source metadata/hints; they must not become generated_news.category.
First-level and second-level labels are the directory for clustering and recommendation; do not add a separate tool management page for that.
Data APIs that list reusable sources may filter by workspace_code through workspace_source_links.
Workspace membership controls access to a desktop, while global roles still control privileged actions.

Daily report item reactions, ratings and comments use workspaces.config_json.feedback_policy: viewer can write only when viewer_can_react/viewer_can_rate/viewer_can_comment is true; member and above can still collaborate when viewer feedback is closed.
viewer_can_edit remains false in v1; report item editing still requires workspace member+.
notify_on_comment is active for daily report comments; notify_on_publish is active for daily_report.published and weekly_report.published notifications and is still off by default.
Changing feedback_policy writes audit_logs with action workspace.feedback_policy.update.
The /users policy view provides a visual editor for viewer_can_react/viewer_can_rate/viewer_can_comment/notify_on_comment/notify_on_publish and requires an explicit impact confirmation before PATCH.
The /users policy view must show feedback_policy before/after diffs from GET /api/identity/permission-changes and may rollback selected changes via POST /api/identity/permission-rollbacks.
The frontend must always send viewer_can_edit=false even if a stale response contains another value.

report_policy.stored_at: workspaces.config_json.report_policy (same level as label_policy and feedback_policy)
rules: When auto_publish_daily is true, the daily pipeline publishes the generated report automatically with actor=system and audit action daily_report.auto_publish (manual publish stays daily_report.publish); the pipeline response carries auto_published.
A request-level auto_publish_daily parameter on POST /api/pipeline/daily-runs may override the workspace policy (null follows the policy).
Auto publish does not bypass publish guards: fallback drafts stay fallback_needs_review and company SQL export gating is unchanged.
Changing report_policy writes audit_logs with action workspace.report_policy.update.
The policy is edited from the workspace settings center (/workspace-settings); post-publish revision semantics live in docs/backend/reports-editorial-design.md section 7.2.

schedule_policy.status: implemented (2026-07-08): read/write API in backend/app/api/routes/workspaces.py, per-workspace scheduler tick + run-level auto-retry in backend/app/workers/scheduler.py + backend/app/pipeline/runs.py, heartbeat via GET /api/pipeline/scheduler/status; covered by backend/tests/test_scheduler_policy.py and test_pipeline_retry.py. Spec: docs/backend/pipeline-jobs-design.md sections 6.1-6.2 and 8
schedule_policy.endpoint_read: GET /api/workspaces/{workspace_code}/schedule-policy (workspace viewer+; returns policy + resolved effective values + next_run_at preview)
field_rules: enabled: null follows the instance master switch INGESTION_SCHEDULER_ENABLED; false opts the workspace out; true is only effective while the instance master switch is on (workspace policy can never override a disabled instance or a DEPLOY_MODE that disables ingestion).
daily_time: null follows INGESTION_SCHEDULER_DAILY_TIME; otherwise HH:MM in the single instance timezone INGESTION_SCHEDULER_TIMEZONE (workspaces cannot override the timezone).
day_offset: null follows DAILY_PIPELINE_DAY_OFFSET_DAYS; otherwise int in -7..0.
source_types: null follows the instance scheduler source types; a non-null list is still filtered by the deployment-level INGESTION_SOURCE_TYPES allowlist.

retry.max_attempts (0..5, default 1) and retry.backoff_seconds (60..21600, default 900) drive run-level pipeline auto-retry with exponential backoff; partial runs never trigger run-level retry and non-retryable error codes (published_report_conflict, capability_disabled, invalid_parameters, workspace_not_found) go terminal immediately.
weekly.enabled/weekly_day (ISO 1..7)/weekly_time schedule an idempotent weekly draft build (workspace_code + week_key); weekly publish stays a manual editorial decision.
Out-of-range values answer 422; changes write audit_logs action workspace.schedule_policy.update with before/after snapshots.

Scheduler behavior: when no workspace has a schedule_policy the scheduler behaves exactly as today (single INGESTION_SCHEDULER_WORKSPACE_CODE); scheduler heartbeat/next-run/last-runs are observable via GET /api/pipeline/scheduler/status.

generation_policy.status: implemented (2026-07-08): policy read/write + POST /api/generation/ping in backend/app/api/routes/generation.py, GENERATION_* env family with MINIMAX_* per-field fallback in backend/app/core/config.py, startup fail-fast in backend/app/core/deploy_checks.py; covered by backend/tests/test_generation_provider.py. Spec: docs/backend/generation-provider-design.md. R2 revision (designed 2026-07-08, implementation pending): credential_id reference into llm_provider_credentials plus provider preset catalog; see config/contracts/llm_providers.json and generation-provider-design.md sections 8-10.
generation_policy.endpoint_read: GET /api/workspaces/{workspace_code}/generation-policy (workspace viewer+; response adds resolved provider/model/base_url_host/key_configured/key_source and, R2, credential_id/credential_label plus credential_options for workspace admin+; never the key itself)
generation_policy.ping_endpoint: POST /api/generation/ping (super_admin or editor_admin; minimal live probe, audited as generation.ping, no secrets in detail; R2 adds optional credential_id body field taking precedence over workspace_code)
field_rules: credential_id (R2, implementation pending): null = instance env chain; otherwise a llm_provider_credentials global_id. PATCH validates the row exists and is enabled (else 422); a later disabled/removed/undecryptable credential degrades to key-not-configured (key_source=credential_missing) and never silently falls back to env. Resolution priority: credential -> instance env (see config/contracts/llm_providers.json resolution_priority).
null fields inherit the instance env defaults (GENERATION_MODEL/GENERATION_TEMPERATURE/GENERATION_MAX_TOKENS/GENERATION_TIMEOUT_SECONDS).

The api key plaintext never enters workspace policy, Git, sync feeds or API responses. Key storage is instance-level: env (GENERATION_API_KEY or GENERATION_API_KEY_REF with env:VAR|file:/path; legacy MINIMAX_* per-field fallback kept) or, per decision change D-2026-07-08-KEY, Fernet-encrypted rows in llm_provider_credentials managed via UI by super_admin (masked ****last4 display only).

daily_generation_budget (1..1000 or null=unlimited) caps model calls per workspace per day_key, including per-item template formatting calls (report_renditions.json generation_template); once recommendation_ranking.json's generation_daily_usage purpose column lands, this budget governs only the purpose=generation bucket (rerank and rubric_compile have their own quotas and never consume it); exhaustion is counted as generation_budget_exhausted in the run summary and remaining items follow fallback_behavior (base drafts) or template_fallback (template formatting).

fallback_behavior: rule_fallback (default, current behavior: rule_v1 draft with fallback_needs_review, excluded from company SQL) | fail (no fallback draft; generation step failed, item can be regenerated later). It applies to base-draft generation only; template formatting failures always degrade to template_fallback projection.

Secret-like keys inside the policy payload answer 422 (privacy.py detection; credential_id is a whitelisted pointer field); changes write audit_logs action workspace.generation_policy.update with before/after snapshots.
Provider or credential choice never relaxes generation quality gates, the 10-category constraint or company SQL gating.

resolution_order: 2. associated domain pack (config/domain_packs/*.json, matched through workspaces.default_domain_code)
3. built-in AI default (identical to the planning_intel rules; keeps existing AI workspaces byte-for-byte unchanged)
resolved_fields: scoring mode and prior keywords (AI workspaces keep the original noise rules + global content scorer; other workspaces use neutral admission with pack/policy priors and no AI noise down-weighting)
category fallback keyword rules (built-in AI/ai_tools keyword tables are the built-in default data)
rendition board taxonomy (ai_builtin/domain_pack/label_policy/single_group; body_json records board_taxonomy_source)
generation fallback persona text (defaults to workspace.name; planning_intel is locked to the original 规划部 wording)
content_policy_resolution.domain_pack_listing_endpoint: GET /api/domain-packs lists consumable packs (boards/label_sets/category_keywords/scoring summary; associate_by=default_domain_code)
content_policy_resolution.company_sql_gating: Company SQL export is only available when the resolved policy is company-SQL capable: news_format_code=company_sql_v1 and allowed_primary_categories is a subset of the AI 10 categories. Other workspaces get preflight report-level error workspace_not_company_sql and HTTP 400 from the export APIs (batch entries fail per item); there is no silent category remapping.

domain_pack_example.rule: The startup seed reads flat JSON files in config/domain_packs and registers label_sets plus labels; domain pack config must not fork the pipeline. Packs are consumed at runtime through content_policy_resolution (scoring priors, boards with fallback_board, category_keywords) and listed by GET /api/domain-packs; consumption semantics are documented in config/domain_packs/README.md.
